zh.Newhouse Abbey, Brockelsby, West Lindsey: 英國英格蘭林可爾史爾
zh.of, Goxhill, Lincolnshire, England: 英國英格蘭林可爾史爾格爾
zh.Camoys Manor, Toppesfield, Essex, Englan: 英國艾可托培斯費爾德
zh.lifeSummary: 當Philip le Despenser於1313年4月6日出生在英國林可爾史爾格爾時，他的父親Philip Despenser23歲，母親Margaret Goushill18歲。他於1341年與Joan Cobham在英國林可爾史爾格爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1349年8月22日在英國艾可托培斯費爾德享年36歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾。

zh-hans.Camoys Manor, Toppesfield, Essex, Englan: 英国艾可托培斯費爾德
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Philip le Despenser于1313年4月6日出生在英国林可爾史爾格爾时，他的父亲Philip Despenser23岁，母亲Margaret Goushill18岁。他于1341年与Joan Cobham在英国林可爾史爾格爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1349年8月22日在英国艾可托培斯費爾德享年36岁，埋葬在英国英格兰林可爾史爾。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Philip
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Greek name Philippos, meaning ‘lover of horses’, from philein ‘to love’ + hippos ‘horse’. This was popular in the classical period and since. It was the name of the father of Alexander the Great . It was also the name of one of Christ's apostles, of a deacon ordained by the apostles after the death of Christ, and of several other early saints. See also Philippa .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Philip le Despenser was born on 6 April 1313, in Goxhill, Lincolnshire, England, his father, Philip Despenser, was 23 and his mother, Margaret Goushill, was 18. He married Joan Cobham in 1341, in Goxhill, Lincolnshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 22 August 1349, in Toppesfield, Essex, England, at the age of 36, and was buried in Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Philip le Despenser est né le 6 avril 1313, à Goxhill, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, son père, Philip Despenser, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Margaret Goushill, avait 18 ans. Il a épousé Joan Cobham en 1341, à Goxhill, Lincolnshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 22 août 1349, à Toppesfield, Essex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 36 ans, et a été enterré à Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Philip le Despenser am 6. April 1313 in Goxhill, Lincolnshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Philip Despenser 23 und seine Mutter Margaret Goushill 18. Er hat 1341 in Goxhill, Lincolnshire, England Joan Cobham geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 22. August 1349 im Alter von 36 Jahren in Toppesfield, Essex, England und wurde in Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Philip le Despenser è nato il il 6 aprile 1313 nel Goxhill, Lincolnshire, England, il suo padre, Philip Despenser, aveva 23 anni e il suo madre, Margaret Goushill, aveva 18 anni. Lui sposato Joan Cobham nel 1341, nel Goxhill, Lincolnshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 22 agosto 1349, nel Toppesfield, Essex, England, all'età di 36 anni, e fu sepolto a Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Philip le Despenserが1313年4月6日にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアゴクスヒルで生まれた時，父Philip Despenserは23歳，母Margaret Goushillは18歳であった。1341年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアゴクスヒルでJoan Cobhamと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1349年8月22日にイギリスエセックストップエスフィールドで36歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Philip le Despenser(이)가 1313년 4월 6일 영국 링코실르 고스힐에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Philip Despenser(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Goushill(은)는 18세였다. 그는 1341년 영국 링코실르 고스힐에서 Joan Cobham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1349년 8월 22일 영국 에세스 토페스필드에서 36세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르에 묻혔다.

pt.6 April 1313–22 August 1349: 6 de abril de 1313–22 de agosto de 1349
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Philip le Despenser nasceu em 6 de abril de 1313, em Goxhill, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Philip Despenser, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Goushill, tinha 18 anos. Ele casou-se com Joan Cobham em 1341, em Goxhill, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 22 de agosto de 1349, em Toppesfield, Essex, Inglaterra, com 36 anos, e foi sepultado em Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Philip le Despenser родился 6 апреля 1313 г. в местности гоксхилл, линколншир, Англия, его отцу по имени Philip Despenser было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Margaret Goushill, 18. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan Cobham в 1341 г. в местности гоксхилл, линколншир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 22 августа 1349 г. в местности топпесфиелд, эссекс, Англия в возрасте 36 лет и был похоронен в местности линколншир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.6 April 1313–22 August 1349: 6 de abril de 1313–22 de agosto de 1349
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Philip le Despenser nació el 6 de abril de 1313, en Goxhill, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Philip Despenser, tenía 23 años y su mamá, Margaret Goushill, tenía 18 años. Se casó con Joan Cobham en 1341, en Goxhill, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 22 de agosto de 1349, en Toppesfield, Essex, Inglaterra, a la edad de 36 años, y fue sepultado en Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
